_comment: Real MCP wiring grounded in vendor docs as of 2026-05-09. Two of four canonical sources (RevenueCat, Stripe) ship MCPs that are configuration-focused and DO NOT expose a date-range events list. We document the gap rather than fake a tool name. PostHog and Sentry both support what we need.

_caveats.revenuecat: Official MCP at mcp.revenuecat.ai exposes list-customer-* tools but no batch events-by-date-range endpoint. For real backfill, supplement with webhooks. The entry below targets list-customer-purchases per app_user_id and assumes you already know which users to look up — useful for the per-user retention loop but not for cold backfill.
_caveats.stripe: Official MCP (npx @stripe/mcp) exposes list_subscriptions, list_customers, list_invoices, list_disputes etc. but no list_events / events.list equivalent. The entry below pulls subscription state changes; payment failures and refunds will need a separate REST/webhook path until Stripe adds events to the MCP.

sources.label: subscriptions
sources._about: RevenueCat — per-customer purchase history. Limitation: not a batch backfill; lists purchases for one app_user_id at a time. Set MCP_SUBSCRIPTIONS_ARGS in your env (or args below) to the user_id you want to inspect, OR write a wrapper that fans out across users. For full lifecycle coverage prefer RC webhooks.

toolArgs.app_user_id: <replace with the user id you want, or remove and call iteratively>

sources.hint: RevenueCat per-customer purchase history. user_id should map from app_user_id (NOT from the RC subscriber id). Fields commonly seen: app_user_id, purchased_at (ISO), product_id, store. Map purchases to subscription.purchase, missing renewals/cancels won't be in this stream — they live on the subscription object.

sources.label: billing
sources._about: Stripe — subscription state. Limitation: official MCP has no list_events tool, so payment.failure/charge.refunded events are NOT reachable via this entry. We pull list_subscriptions to catch active/canceled state; supplement with webhooks for full payment lifecycle.
sources.hint: Stripe Subscription objects. user_id should be resolved from customer.metadata.app_user_id when present; if you've configured the MCP server with a fixed customer_id arg, use that instead. Map status=active recent → subscription.purchase or subscription.renewal, status=canceled → subscription.cancel. Use created (Unix seconds) or current_period_start for the timestamp.

sources.label: engagement
sources._about: PostHog — raw event stream via HogQL. This works as designed. The args.query below pulls the last 60 days of session events; tweak the WHERE clause if you also want custom usage events. The MCP returns rows; the LLM normalizer maps each row to user_id/timestamp/kind.

toolArgs.query: SELECT distinct_id, properties.app_user_id AS app_user_id, event, timestamp, properties FROM events WHERE event IN ('$session_start', '$pageview') AND timestamp >= now() - INTERVAL 60 DAY ORDER BY timestamp DESC LIMIT 10000

sources.hint: PostHog HogQL rows. user_id should be properties.app_user_id when present, else distinct_id. timestamp is ISO. Map event='$session_start' to usage.session, '$pageview' or any custom name to usage.feature. Skip $identify/$set rows.

sources.label: errors
sources._about: Sentry — error/crash events via the hosted MCP (OAuth handshake handled by the MCP client). The search_events tool with dataset='errors' returns per-event records with user context.
sources.hint: Sentry events. user_id from user.id (fall back to user.email or user.username). timestamp is ISO. Map level='fatal' OR exception.values[].mechanism.handled=false to error.crash; level='error' to error.client. Skip events without a user identifier.
